author: Jay
text: I went to Paik’s Noodle in Amsterdam and really enjoyed it! The jajangmyeon and yuringi were tasty and satisfying. The bokkeum jjamppong was also tasty, but it honestly felt more like a good chow mein than the spicy Korean-style bokkeum jjamppong I’m used to in Korea. Even though the flavor was quite different, the fresh ingredients and smoky wok flavor were really nice. Overall, it’s a nice place, and I’d definitely come back for the jajangmyeon!

The food itself is fine. Authentic Korean? I don't think so. It is more appealing to Western tourists. The service. I understand and somewhat appreciate their self-service concept. It's convenient to scan the QR code and place the order.

If you can handle spicy food the jjamppong is really good. The fried pork is really tasty but I recommend taking the sauce on the side to keep it crispy for longer. The staff was really polite.

I was also very happy to hear from the actual source the story of when Kim Namjoon of BTS visited the restaurant in June 2025. It was lovely!

author: Tenzin Sangmo
text: I basically turned half-vegetarian for this meal alone. The place didn’t have any official vegetarian options, but the staff were absolute heroes. they customised my tteokbokki without fishcake and even made black bean noodles minus the pork chunks. Honestly, the service is top notch. And the best part? Everything still tasted amazing. Thanks to the team for turning my “uh-oh, what do I eat?” moment into a surprisingly wholesome (and delicious) adventure. You didn’t just serve me food—you gave me a whole story to tell

Also, I came here because RM went here and was very happy to see his signature on the 2nd floor ☺️🫶🏻

The staff were incredibly kind and welcoming, which made the experience even better. Overall, a great place to enjoy a nice meal and fill your stomach. Definitely recommend!

author: Chris C.
text: Paik's Noodle nails that sweet spot between Chinese and Korean flavors - think bold, saucy jjajangmyeon without the fuss. Ordered the same black bean noodles in Amsterdam that I had two years ago in Seoul, and boom: identical quality. No fancy gimmicks, just steaming bowls of comfort that hit hard and fast. Perfect for when you need real food, real quick. Consistency? Flawless. Vibes? Pure efficiency. Don't overthink it - this is fast, flavorful, and exactly what you came for.

author: Samantha Kemmler
text: Even in prior of watching culinary wars on Netflix I was following the launch process of Paik's in NL on social media. So, one could say that it has been a long time since I had wanted to try it. As the walk-in concept wouldn't necessarily suit me and friends going, I opted for a lunch visit by myself. There was a small queue already just in prior of opening and guests quickly filled up the ground level. I opted for the Jajangmyeon & the Tangsuyuk. Everything was being prepared freshly and it took my order roughly 20mins to be served. The staff was friendly and accommodating to explain everything whereas needed to customers. The taste of the food was superb. Exactly what I was hoping for. It just gave "comfort food"-vibes. I would even say that I liked the Tangsuyuk the best. For my liking (though I mixed everything well to have all the noodles coated), it was a bit much of the back bean sauce but that's a personal preference. The only downside in my opinion are the costs. Overall it suits the price levels you would expect in Amsterdam but 19€ for a serving of just Tangsuyuk is a bit high in my opinion. Yet one has to say that you wouldn't find these dishes (regularly) on any other menu in or around Amsterdam. Will I come back? Yes. Will it be soon at this price point, probably not.

author: Jack Lyne
text: I have been here multiple times, and I recommend their black bean noodles and fried pork combination more than the jjampong. The portions are really huge and the noodles fill you up quick. I had to wait a while to get my food (~ 15 minutes) even though it wasn’t being busy.

author: Ebi
text: Reasonable price in Amsterdam center. The portion size was ok.. I saw other tables also order the shared noodles for 2p (25euros), people almost licked the dish. The deep fried pork (19euros) was crispy but the batter was too thick. …

author: Patrick Donghwi Park
text: Discovering authentic Korean-style Chinese cuisine in Europe can often be a challenging endeavour, as this specific fusion is not as widely available as traditional Chinese or Korean food individually. However, I recently had the pleasure of finding a restaurant in Amsterdam, Paik's Noodle Amsterdam, which offers this particular culinary experience. Based on my visit, where I ordered Jajangmyeon (black bean noodles) and Tangsuyuk (sweet and sour pork), I can confirm that the taste remarkably replicates the flavour one would expect to find in Korea. The Tangsuyuk was particularly well-executed. The pieces of pork were coated in a batter that achieved a desirable crispness on the outside, while the meat within remained tender and substantial. The sweet and sour sauce provided a balanced accompaniment, complementing the texture and flavour of the fried pork without being overly dominant. The Jajangmyeon also met expectations. The noodles had a good texture, being appropriately firm and not at all soggy, which is crucial for this dish. The black bean sauce itself was flavourful and well-prepared, adhering nicely to the noodles and delivering that distinctive, savoury taste.

author: K.L.
text: As a Korean living here in the Netherlands, I can say the food is just how Koreans like. There was a review below that said this food is catered to Westerners, but I have to disagree. The food is delicious and comes out quickly after ordering. The food is pricier than chains in the U.S., but as an occasional visit I can justify this for myself. The staff are always so friendly, especially the young lady with the glasses and dark hair. I visited today with my baby stroller, and the staff was welcoming and accommodating since the space isn’t the biggest. A suggestion though is to provide a baby diaper changing table, though the staff (again) were accommodating and offered a space to change my child. I will keep coming to this restaurant and fully recommend it. Food is delicious and staff are so pleasant.

author: Jungwon H
text: We are a Korean family who have lived in Europe for almost two decades, while returning to Korea at least once a year. So we know very well what Korean-Chinese food should taste like, and we also understand what to realistically expect in Europe. Unfortunately, this was by far the worst experience we’ve had. Even our seven-year-old, who was very hungry, refused to eat the food. The jjamppong soup was strangely sour, and the vegetables were completely soggy, as if they had been sitting in the broth for far too long. There was hardly any seafood. The noodles in the jajangmyeon were overcooked and mushy. Given the prices, we expected either better food or better service. The service felt almost self-service, cafeteria-style. If there were an option to give zero stars, I would. That said, the staff were friendly, and the tangsuyuk was slightly better than the other dishes, but overall, I would avoid. As this was not a question of authenticity. We are fully aware that Korean-Chinese food in Europe may differ from what we have in Korea. The real issue was not whether it was perfectly authentic, but whether the food was prepared with professionalism, care, and basic culinary quality. In this case, it simply did not feel that attention or standard was there.

author: Generalarry
text: Despite the reviews and some comments, we decided to give this celebrity chef’s restaurant a try. Honestly we enjoyed it! The jajangmyeon was delicious and the tantsuyuk was pretty good! Obviously we aren’t in Korea, but for a Korea restaurant in Amsterdam it’s pretty tasty! Staff was also friendly and the ordering system was easy to use and convenient!

author: Tara Hajimoto
text: I come here often, price is really good for what you get especially here in the city center and also authentic, unlike many other Korean restaurants in the city center. I saw some reviewers commenting it’s not authentic, but everytime I go there, most customers are Koreans, it’s like being back in South-Korea. I also don’t understand why some reviewers say it’s expensive. It’s Amsterdam and even a simple sandwich is around the same price. The last time I went for the Jjajangmyeon and Tangsuyuk, they were delicious. Service is very attentive and helpful, you order with QR menu, which is very simple and efficient, once my phone was dead, you can ask the staff to place an order also. Once my Jjajangmyeon was a bit not warm enough, but they made me a new one, they solved that well. I heard RM from BTS ate there last summer and he finished all the food, I was glad the staff shared it to me after I said I loved BTS and they also showed me his signature in the restaurant. I will come back for sure.

author: Vincent
text: In the heart of Amsterdam Paik’s Noodles opened a new restaurant serving authentic Korean-Chinese cuisine. The menu is small, but every dish we tried was spot on. We started with their famous Jajang Myeon, which was packed with rich, authentic flavors. The Jjampong came next, the noodles were perfectly bouncy though I wouldn’t have minded a bit more seafood. The Tang Suyuk was also a highlight, crispy and flavorful. Portions were generous, and they even offer an option to upsize for a few extra euro’s. The staff was friendly and efficient, and the clean, tidy interior made for a pleasant dining atmosphere. Though there was a small queue, it moved quickly, and the prices were very reasonable for the quality and portion sizes. I will definitely be back and highly recommend it to anyone looking for a satisfying meal!

text: I've been here three times now and I just keep going back for the black bean noodles. I've tried most other dishes and while they are tasty, the noodles definitely take the cake. It's not the type of place designed for hanging around for a longer period but in terms of price quality it's a good place for food. You don't have a lot of interaction with the staff since you order through qr code but they all seem very friendly. I can also recommend getting the pickled vegetables at the self service bar.

The restaurant has seating on two floors, aprons which you might need when eating the jajangmyeon. They have self service for the pickles/onions that you can eat with the dishes you order. Ordering and payment was done through the QR codes on the tables. After that it’s just waiting for your food to arrive. Definitely workgroup visiting again for lunch once we’re back in Amsterdam on another day.

author: Zil
text: If you’re looking for a culinary experience that’s both unique and unforgettable, Paik’s Noodle Amsterdam is an absolute must-visit. As someone who has never been to Korea, I found the flavors here to be completely new to me—and absolutely delicious. Every bite felt like a revelation! We were seated upstairs. I had the Jajangmyeon, which is Korean black bean sauce noodles with stir-fried pork and vegetables. Normally, I’m not a fan of pork, but this dish was so flavorful that I couldn’t stop eating it. The sauce was rich, savory, and perfectly balanced—it was unlike anything I’ve tasted before. I also shared the Gochu Yuringi, a dish of deep-fried chicken topped with sweet-and-sour soy sauce and hot chili peppers. No exaggeration: this was the best chicken I’ve ever had. The crispy coating, tender meat, and flavorful sauce worked so well together that it felt like a masterpiece on a plate. I can’t recommend it enough! What stood out to me was how approachable the food was—it was incredibly easy and enjoyable to eat, making it perfect for anyone visiting the restaurant, whether they’re familiar with Korean cuisine or not. There were also extra sauces and flavours on every table, so everyone can adjust the taste of their dishes. The service was very friendly and accommodating. I first learned about Paik through his appearance on Culinary Class Wars on Netflix, and his passion for making food accessible and delicious really shines through in the dishes here. He’s not just a chef—he’s a storyteller who brings Korean (and Chinese) cuisine to life. Thank you, Paik’s Noodle Amsterdam, for an amazing dining experience I’ll never forget. And thank you, Paik, for sharing your love of food and culture with the world! I’ll definitely be back.

author: Hyunji Kim
text: I have to say that my expectation was lower after seeing some reviews here, but I personally liked it very much. I ordered the spicy seafood noodle soup and finished it so quickly because it was delicious - the taste I remember from S.Korea. The noodle was thicker than usual, but the taste was well blended in the soup. The atmosphere is more like a fast food place with bright white lighting. But I had a very nice meal and felt comfy & great afterwards. I'll defo visit again!

text: N.B. I am Korean, with extremely picky taste... I understand the 짬뽕 국물 had to be toned down a bit to appeal to foreigners, but the noodles used for both 짜장면 and 짬뽕 didn't seem to be made out of flours. I don't know what the issue was, but the noodles were dark and had a dry taste. 탕수육 was done properly - chewy and well done. 탕수육 추천 합니다.

text: We went on Saturday noon around 1pm. Waited just a bit for the table being prepared. Ordered through table scan app. The waitress was friendly. The food came fast. We had jajangmyeon, gochu jjamppong and Tangsuyuk for share. Portion is good enough for one person, maybe if you are small eater, slightly in the bigger size. The price vs quantity is reasonable. The condiments are self service, reminded me when had jajangmyeon at Dangnaemun market. No free refillable cold water like in Seoul, but for €0.80 you can have refillable tap water, and cold. I have got an advice on how to properly eat Jajangmyeon, from the restaurant guy. So it needs to be throughly stirred, the sauce and noodles. I do like the place. Not much choices on which makes it easy to make choice and whatever choice will deliver to you yummy foods. Tangsuyuk is very recommendable. I like the texture of the noodles.

Thanks to the team for turning my “uh-oh, what do I eat?” moment into a surprisingly wholesome (and delicious) adventure. You didn’t just serve me food—you gave me a whole story to tell

author: Nhi Dobber
text: The jajangmyeon was very delicious! We loved the texture of the noodles 🫶🏻 Tangsuyuk wasn’t really our thing. The first few bites were delicious but at some point the sauce on top was too sweet for me and the texture of the outside layer of the pork was too chewy for my husband. Still enjoy both dishes very much! The portions were big and these 2 dishes were more than enough for us. I like the self-service concept and it was good that the staff confirmed if we’ve been here before so they can explain how things work. Also, I came here because RM went here and was very happy to see his signature on the 2nd floor ☺️🫶🏻

author: Lynn Roesiani
text: We had a quick lunch at Paik’s Noodle, the service was fast and it was nice that there was a self service bar for the side dishes. We ordered a big portion of jjajangmyeon to share as well as tansuyuk. The jjajangmyeon was tasty, but the tansuyuk was too sweet for us.

author: Ziba Kashefi
text: We tried the jjamppong, jajangmyeon and tangsuyuk. The jjampomg was good, spicy and bit but nothing super special. The tangusyuk was probably my favorite and then the jajangmyeon. They were fresh and delicious! Portions are quite the right amount. They also have 2 side dishes. There were a lot of fruit flies everywhere which is quite underwhelming. staff are nice as well.

author: Isabella
text: It was mediocre Korean food. Did not taste too authentic but also not bad. We had black bean noodles and tangsuyuk which were okay.

author: W X
text: 1 star for the chicken which was alright but lacked sauce. Half of the menu was out. The noodles were made in 1 minute, soggy and overcooked. Sauce didn’t convince. I regretted that dish so much. Overall not a nice experience.

author: RiceFieldz
text: Food was good. The jjajangmyeon was tasty. Tteokbokki was good but not really warm. Prices are fair and not that expensive. Overall good fast casual spot.

text: Great vibe right in the center of Amsterdam. Authentic Korean-Chinese cuisine. Highly recommending Bokkeum Jjamppong, Yang Nyum Chicken and Jajangmyeon. Deliver flavor bombs with speed and consistency. The dishes are unapologetically bold, portions generous, and prices wallet-friendly. Not far from Rockin Metro Station.

author: Hakan Yalcintas
text: I visited this restaurant while in the Netherlands and absolutely loved it! The noodles were delicious — full of flavour and perfectly cooked. The staff were incredibly kind and welcoming, which made the experience even better. Overall, a great place to enjoy a nice meal and fill your stomach. Definitely recommend!

All that costed 49 euros (36 without the little bottle of soju to share) Highly recommended! Good food and cute vibes. Also, clean and nice noise volume inside. You can talk (not overwhelming noise at all)

author: Chris C.
text: Paik's Noodle nails that sweet spot between Chinese and Korean flavors - think bold, saucy jjajangmyeon without the fuss. Ordered the same black bean noodles in Amsterdam that I had two years ago in Seoul, and boom: identical quality. No fancy gimmicks, just steaming bowls of comfort that hit hard and fast. Perfect for when you need real food, real quick. Consistency? Flawless. Vibes? Pure efficiency. Don't overthink it - this is fast, flavorful, and exactly what you came for.

author: Generalarry
text: Despite the reviews and some comments, we decided to give this celebrity chef’s restaurant a try. Honestly we enjoyed it! The jajangmyeon was delicious and the tantsuyuk was pretty good! Obviously we aren’t in Korea, but for a Korea restaurant in Amsterdam it’s pretty tasty! Staff was also friendly and the ordering system was easy to use and convenient!

author: Axo Lotl
text: I had the spicy chicken and it was really tasty! I recommend this place. Very authentic Korean food 😋 …

author: dutch방콕녀들
text: Finally, I got this restaurant in Amsterdam. I ordered various dishes. That has a real Korean taste, and of course, they were so delicious. Especially, I love deep-fried chicken in hot and Sour Soy sauce. If you visit here, you need to try it.

author: Samantha Kemmler
text: Even in prior of watching culinary wars on Netflix I was following the launch process of Paik's in NL on social media. So, one could say that it has been a long time since I had wanted to try it. As the walk-in concept wouldn't necessarily …

author: Tara Hajimoto
text: I come here often, price is really good for what you get especially here in the city center and also authentic, unlike many other Korean restaurants in the city center. I saw some reviewers commenting it’s not authentic, but everytime I go there, most customers are Koreans, it’s like being back in South-Korea. I also don’t understand why some reviewers say it’s expensive. It’s Amsterdam and even a simple sandwich is around the same price. The last time I went for the Jjajangmyeon and Tangsuyuk, they were delicious. Service is very attentive and helpful, you order with QR menu, which is very simple and efficient, once my phone was dead, you can ask the staff to place an order also. Once my Jjajangmyeon was a bit not warm enough, but they made me a new one, they solved that well. I heard RM from BTS ate there last summer and he finished all the food, I was glad the staff shared it to me after I said I loved BTS and they also showed me his signature in the restaurant. I will come back for sure.

author: Jack Lyne
text: I have been here multiple times, and I recommend their black bean noodles and fried pork combination more than the jjampong. The portions are really huge and the noodles fill you up quick. I had to wait a while to get my food (~ 15 minutes) even though it wasn’t being busy.

author: Ebi
text: Reasonable price in Amsterdam center. The portion size was ok.. I saw other tables also order the shared noodles for 2p (25euros), people almost licked the dish. The deep fried pork (19euros) was crispy but the batter was too thick. You can order and pay directly online. It’s nice that they offer tap water for 80 cents.

author: Patrick Donghwi Park

author: K.L.
text: As a Korean living here in the Netherlands, I can say the food is just how Koreans like. There was a review below that said this food is catered to Westerners, but I have to disagree. The food is delicious and comes out quickly after ordering. The food is pricier than chains in the U.S., but as an occasional visit I can justify this for myself. The staff are always so friendly, especially the young lady with the glasses and dark hair. I visited today with my baby stroller, and the staff was welcoming and accommodating since the space isn’t the biggest. A suggestion though is to provide a baby diaper changing table, though the staff (again) were accommodating and offered a space to change my child. I will keep coming to this restaurant and fully recommend it. Food is delicious and staff are so pleasant.

author: Jungwon H
text: We are a Korean family who have lived in Europe for almost two decades, while returning to Korea at least once a year. So we know very well what Korean-Chinese food should taste like, and we also understand what to realistically expect in Europe. Unfortunately, this was by far the worst experience we’ve had. Even our seven-year-old, who was very hungry, refused to eat the food. The jjamppong soup was strangely sour, and the vegetables were completely soggy, as if they had been sitting in the broth for far too long. There was hardly any seafood. The noodles in the jajangmyeon were overcooked and mushy. Given the prices, we expected either better food or better service. The service felt almost self-service, cafeteria-style. If there were an option to give zero stars, I would. That said, the staff were friendly, and the tangsuyuk was slightly better than the other dishes, but overall, I would avoid. As this was not a question of authenticity. We are fully aware that Korean-Chinese food in Europe may differ from what we have in Korea. The real issue was not whether it was perfectly authentic, but whether the food was prepared with professionalism, care, and basic culinary quality. In this case, it simply did not feel that attention or standard was there.

author: Samantha Kemmler
text: Even in prior of watching culinary wars on Netflix I was following the launch process of Paik's in NL on social media. So, one could say that it has been a long time since I had wanted to try it. As the walk-in concept wouldn't necessarily suit me and friends going, I opted for a lunch visit by myself. There was a small queue already just in prior of opening and guests quickly filled up the ground level. I opted for the Jajangmyeon & the Tangsuyuk. Everything was being prepared freshly and it took my order roughly 20mins to be served. The staff was friendly and accommodating to explain everything whereas needed to customers. The taste of the food was superb. Exactly what I was hoping for. It just gave "comfort food"-vibes. I would even say that I liked the Tangsuyuk the best. For my liking (though I mixed everything well to have all the noodles coated), it was a bit much of the back bean sauce but that's a personal preference. The only downside in my opinion are the costs. Overall it suits the price levels you would expect in Amsterdam but 19€ for a serving of just Tangsuyuk is a bit high in my opinion. Yet one has to say that you wouldn't find these dishes (regularly) on any other menu in or around Amsterdam. Will I come back? Yes. Will it be soon at this price point, probably not.

author: Patrick Donghwi Park
text: Discovering authentic Korean-style Chinese cuisine in Europe can often be a challenging endeavour, as this specific fusion is not as widely available as traditional Chinese or Korean food individually. However, I recently had the pleasure of finding a restaurant in Amsterdam, Paik's Noodle Amsterdam, which offers this particular culinary experience. Based on my visit, where I ordered Jajangmyeon (black bean noodles) and Tangsuyuk (sweet and sour pork), I can confirm that the taste remarkably replicates the flavour one would expect to find in Korea. The Tangsuyuk was particularly well-executed. The pieces of pork were coated in a batter that achieved a desirable crispness on the outside, while the meat within remained tender and substantial. The sweet and sour sauce provided a balanced accompaniment, complementing the texture and flavour of the fried pork without being overly dominant. The Jajangmyeon also met expectations. The noodles had a good texture, being appropriately firm and not at all soggy, which is crucial for this dish. The black bean sauce itself was flavourful and well-prepared, adhering nicely to the noodles and delivering that distinctive, savoury taste.

author: Vincent
text: In the heart of Amsterdam Paik’s Noodles opened a new restaurant serving authentic Korean-Chinese cuisine. The menu is small, but every dish we tried was spot on. We started with their famous Jajang Myeon, which was packed with rich, …

text: Loved the Tangsuyuk and the Jjajangmyeon Ordering takes place through the QR code but staff will explain how it works. the Tangsuyuk pairs well with the fatty jjajang sauce i totally enjoyed eating it. …

author: Hsiao Ming Fan
text: I've been here three times now and I just keep going back for the black bean noodles. I've tried most other dishes and while they are tasty, the noodles definitely take the cake. It's not the type of place designed for hanging around for a longer period but in terms of price quality it's a good place for food. You don't have a lot of interaction with the staff since you order through qr code but they all seem very friendly. I can also recommend getting the pickled vegetables at the self service bar.

author: Shirley Zheng
text: We went there on a Sunday afternoon at 16.00 thinking there would be no wait, ended up waiting 1.5 hours to get in. Worth the wait! But, I would definitely come back once the hype dies down a bit. I was quite hangry towards the end. …

author: G. K

author: akil komur
text: We had an early dinner here last weekend. It’s been almost 10 years ago since we had jajangmyeong in Seoul and since we saw that a shop opened up in Amsterdam we had to go try it. Besides the jajang we had the tangsuyuk and the jampong. All three dishes are quite good. I would be willing to pay extra if they had an upgraded seafood in the jampong. The restaurant has seating on two floors, aprons which you might need when eating the jajangmyeon. They have self service for the pickles/onions that you can eat with the dishes you order. Ordering and payment was done through the QR codes on the tables. After that it’s just waiting for your food to arrive. Definitely workgroup visiting again for lunch once we’re back in Amsterdam on another day.

author: Gretta Zhang
text: The food seems to be not fresh made. Feels like they had been made one hour ago and then reheated. Pork and eggs are very very dry. …

author: M Lin
text: Very yummy food with well-balanced flavors. Nothing too overpowering. I do suggest adding vinegar and chilli to the jajangmyeon. It truly elevates the dish. The korean fried chicken (yangnyeom) is top-tier. Def try it.

author: Silan Isik
text: Unfortunately it was a huge disappointment… First of all I was expecting maybe egg or cucumber options for on top of jajangmyeons. Also there was no vegetarian/vegan/helal options!! In the menu they say they cant change anything based on your diet. So customer unfriendly! Korean fried chicken’s sauce was so boring after a few bites. I wouldnt recommend

author: Axo Lotl
text: I had the spicy chicken and it was really tasty! I recommend this place. Very authentic Korean food 😋

author: Vincent
text: In the heart of Amsterdam Paik’s Noodles opened a new restaurant serving authentic Korean-Chinese cuisine. The menu is small, but every dish we tried was spot on. We started with their famous Jajang Myeon, which was packed with rich, authentic flavors. The Jjampong came next, the noodles were perfectly bouncy though I wouldn’t have minded a bit more seafood. The Tang Suyuk was also a highlight, crispy and flavorful. Portions were generous, and they even offer an option to upsize for a few extra euro’s. The staff was friendly and efficient, and the clean, tidy interior made for a pleasant dining atmosphere. Though there was a small queue, it moved quickly, and the prices were very reasonable for the quality and portion sizes. I will definitely be back and highly recommend it to anyone looking for a satisfying meal!

author: Shirley Zheng
text: We went there on a Sunday afternoon at 16.00 thinking there would be no wait, ended up waiting 1.5 hours to get in. Worth the wait! But, I would definitely come back once the hype dies down a bit. I was quite hangry towards the end. Moving on to the food. The menu is small, and we basically got one of everything. We tried the noodles with black bean, the seafood spicy noodle soup, sweet & sour pork, and the fried chicken. Top hits are the fried chicken with the spicy noodle soup, I would definitely come back for these two dishes. They were delicious! I haven’t had flavor like this in Amsterdam. Looking forward to going back soon!

author: Gretta Zhang
text: The food seems to be not fresh made. Feels like they had been made one hour ago and then reheated. Pork and eggs are very very dry. Do not deserve the money! 💸

author: Calvin de Wilde
text: I am disappointed with the amount of the pork pieces served in the bowl. There were only 4 pieces of pork maximum. This is a dishonest practice and I don't want to come to this place again for the second time. For €15 euro I expected a fair amount of protein, but this one is just an overpriced carbohydrates. The taste is good enough.

author: Y. Wang
text: We have had an great meal! The gochu jajiangmyeon tasted great! For children also good option with jajiangmyeon or jajang bap rice. Gochu Jjamppong was good spicy. We liked it a lot. On the side we had the Tabgsuyuk with the sauce seperate, everything tasted very good! You order and pay through your phone, one off the places in the netherlands where this system does work! Everythign was great, we will definitely return here. Just be aware that there is a big line outside. The restaurant opens at 15:00 and we arrived at 14:30 but there was aready people waiting in front, luckily we decided to wait from 14:30 and could get in the restaurant on time.

I understand the 짬뽕 국물 had to be toned down a bit to appeal to foreigners, but the noodles used for both 짜장면 and 짬뽕 didn't seem to be made out of flours. I don't know what the issue was, but the noodles were dark and had a dry taste. 탕수육 was done properly - chewy and well done. 탕수육 추천 합니다.

text: Very disappointing experience. It was my only bad one in Amsterdam. I was really craving Korean food and was excited to find a branch of my favourite restaurant from Korea. Jjamppong is especially hard to find done well in Europe, so I did not hesitate to visit. To be honest, the service was terrible. The welcome was okay, but upstairs they did not set up anything. No basic table setup, no free side dishes, nothing. It was confusing, especially since they had a signboard and a service table prepared upstairs, but clearly were not using it. The staff member who responded to us was quite rude, and the food was disappointing. I did not even finish half of it. Never again. I would not recommend this place to anyone.

The Jjamppong was spicy as well! I do expected the noodles to be a bit more chewy so maybe a tip for the restaurant to not overcook the noodles too much so it is a bit more al dente?

author: Zoe Kang
text: Highly recommend! We hit the place just before the opening hour, and it got quickly filled up. Of course, the food was great! My kids loved every bits of it, even the reindeer mr. PAIK at the entrance. Very welcoming and very kind, definitely visiting again 😀

author: James Coenraad
text: Today I went to Paiks Noodle in Amsterdam. It serves Korean-Chinese food. There was no queue. We ordered the 'Jajangmyeon' and 'Jjampong'. The serving sizes were decent for the price. The Jajangmyeon was good. It did lack some flavor, like a pinch of salt, even though it was sweet enough because of the onions. It misses some umami. The consistency of the sauce is good and it blends well with the noodles. The noodles were decent, had a good bite, but nothing over the top. I have had better Jajangmyeon in Amsterdam. 3.5/5✨️ This is the first time I tried Jjampong. The Jjampong was better imo, the broth is well seasoned and spicy. If you can not take spicy very well, just go with the regular one. It contains mussels and other greens like cabbage. 4/5✨️ The service is good. You have to order by scanning a qr code and the food was delivered quite fast. In conclusion, it is a good place for eating and trying out Korean food. I would prefer the Jjampong over the Jajangmyeon if you like mild food. If you can eat spicy, try out the Jjampong. Pros: - Price - Fast service Cons: - Can be bland for some - May have to wait in line

author: Jaejun Kim
text: I lived in Europe for 10 years, and it’s always delightful to find Korean-style Chinese food here. I ordered Jajangmyeon, Tangsuyuk, and Jjamppong. The Jajangmyeon and Tangsuyuk tasted very similar to what you’d find in Korea, and I was very satisfied with them. However, the Jjamppong was a bit disappointing. The seasoning didn’t seem quite right, and it was overwhelmingly salty. It might have been an off day when I visited, but I wouldn’t recommend it. For those coming straight from Korea to Amsterdam, I wouldn’t suggest this place. However, for those like me who have lived abroad for a long time, it’s worth a try!

author: Guerlain Nguyen
text: So cozy, quick service and amazing food. Highly recommend their TangSooYook (sweet and sour pork). We’d prefer warmer Jajangmyeon and crispier TangSooYook, as they came out a bit cold. But the taste was quite up to our expectations.

author: Nowy
text: Finally, a Korean chain has opened here in the Netherlands! I was surprised to attend the soft opening of the restaurant today, and the atmosphere and service were amazing, with Korean greetings both upon arrival and departure. The food is affordable and really tasty—I highly recommend the spicy jajangmyeon if you can handle the spice, and the portions are very generous! It’s a great place to dine with friends, and I will definitely come back to try the other dishes.

author: Kathrina Llamas-Lee

author: Andy Kang
text: Discovering this Korean-Chinese establishment with over two decades of history was a delightful surprise. Their Jjajangmyeon is authentic, reminiscent of what you’d find on a local street in Korea. Gochu Yuringi recommended over Tangsuyuk.

author: Tram Bui
text: Very good jjajangmyeon, a little sweet. If you can handle chilies, definitely get the spicy option – there will be lots of sliced chilies but you can mix in or leave out as much as your heart desires. Self serving bar for pickled radish and apron. One minus star because I had to bring my glass to the staff counter for the water refill :( but they refilled others’ at their table so not sure what that was about..??

author: Zil
text: If you’re looking for a culinary experience that’s both unique and unforgettable, Paik’s Noodle Amsterdam is an absolute must-visit. As someone who has never been to Korea, I found the flavors here to be completely new to me—and absolutely delicious. Every bite felt like a revelation! We were seated upstairs. I had the Jajangmyeon, which is Korean black bean sauce noodles with stir-fried pork and vegetables. Normally, I’m not a fan of pork, but this dish was so flavorful that I couldn’t stop eating it. The sauce was rich, savory, and perfectly balanced—it was unlike anything I’ve tasted before. I also shared the Gochu Yuringi, a dish of deep-fried chicken topped with sweet-and-sour soy sauce and hot chili peppers. No exaggeration: this was the best chicken I’ve ever had. The crispy coating, tender meat, and flavorful sauce worked so well together that it felt like a masterpiece on a plate. I can’t recommend it enough! What stood out to me was how approachable the food was—it was incredibly easy and enjoyable to eat, making it perfect for anyone visiting the restaurant, whether they’re familiar with Korean cuisine or not. There were also extra sauces and flavours on every table, so everyone can adjust the taste of their dishes. The service was very friendly and accommodating. I first learned about Paik through his appearance on Culinary Class Wars on Netflix, and his passion for making food accessible and delicious really shines through in the dishes here. He’s not just a chef—he’s a storyteller who brings Korean (and Chinese) cuisine to life. Thank you, Paik’s Noodle Amsterdam, for an amazing dining experience I’ll never forget. And thank you, Paik, for sharing your love of food and culture with the world! I’ll definitely be back.

author: Thao Loo
text: I was so happy to see Paik's Noodle opening in Europe. I already visited it in Korea years ago. Unfortunatly it does not come close to the one in Seoul. The Jiajianmyeon was bland. There was hardly any meat. The portions were big and it was affordable though.

author: YGxETM
text: The chicken is crispy and the sauce is full of flavor. I'll definitely come back 🙃

author: Seunghu Lee
text: I’m really looking forward to eating Jjajangmeon in Amsterdam. Finally, I’ve been there. Compared to what I ate in Korea, I couldn’t find any difference between them. …

author: Blair Seo
text: The noodles were different from the jjajangmyeon I had in Korea, which was a bit disappointing, but it was still very delicious! Would definitely recommend!

author: Sergio
text: I don't understand the hype. The jjamppong was too bland and just tasted spicy. They put literally two mussels, two squid rings and two strips of super tough meat. The jjajang sauce was better, but not really different from what you can …

author: lisbo_oa
text: I have never had such a delicious noodle! The prices are low and I was satisfied! Also I was lucky, I got there around 5pm and there was no one in line. The system is to order and pay by QR code at the table, so you need a table even if you want to take your food to go!

author: Y Oh
text: We arrived at 6pm and could have a table after waiting around 15mins in the queue. ordered dishes via the qr code and the foods were served in 10mins. Satisfied with Impressive taste especially Jjamppong served with huge mussels! And the price’s reasonable.

author: Abril Rios
text: Wow! I could cry! One of my beloved restaurants from Seoul is finally here to save us from all the bad Korean food in Amsterdam! Genuinely so impressed, it tastes exactly how I remember it. If you wanted black bean noodles, you can finally have them! The staff was also so kind and attentive. I will be back often. 10/10

author: F. T.
text: The food is mid, portion seems huge but it’s filled with batter/vege. For €16 seafood jjampong I only got 2 mussels and 1 squid ring and a few pieces of thin pork strands (maybe 5-6 pieces). The €19 tangsuyuk looks big initially but the batter is twice or thrice the size of the meat, and the sauce is so snotty, which just means the sauce is mid at best. Would not return.

author: asdfg hjkl
text: I recently visited Paik's Noodle Amsterdam, hoping for a great Korean meal, but I left feeling a bit let down. I ordered the Jajang Bap, Tangsuyuk, and Jjamppong, expecting vibrant flavors from a well-known restaurant, but the dishes didn’t quite hit the mark. The main issue was finding a hair in the Tangsuyuk. The staff offered to remake the dish, which I know is standard, but I chose to keep it to avoid food waste. They also asked if I’d like a drink to make up for it, which I appreciated, so I just requested tap water. Overall, the staff was polite, but the situation could’ve been handled more smoothly, and the food quality didn’t quite meet my expectations. With plenty of other Korean options around, I’d love to see some improvements that would bring Paik’s Noodle up to the level of quality and service I was hoping for.

author: G. K
text: With high hopes from Paik’s Noodle's reputation (Hong Kong Banjeom in Korea), I left underwhelmed. The jjajangmyeon was overly sweet—lacking the deep umami of traditional black bean sauce. Jjamppong had a harsh MSG aftertaste that overpowered the seafood. Surprisingly, our order took 30+ minutes despite a half-empty restaurant. Only the tangsuyuk (crispy, well-sauced) met expectations. For a Michelin Bib Gourmand spot, improvement is needed

author: Agyys D
text: We went on Saturday noon around 1pm. Waited just a bit for the table being prepared. Ordered through table scan app. The waitress was friendly. The food came fast. We had jajangmyeon, gochu jjamppong and Tangsuyuk for share. Portion is good enough for one person, maybe if you are small eater, slightly in the bigger size. The price vs quantity is reasonable. The condiments are self service, reminded me when had jajangmyeon at Dangnaemun market. No free refillable cold water like in Seoul, but for €0.80 you can have refillable tap water, and cold. I have got an advice on how to properly eat Jajangmyeon, from the restaurant guy. So it needs to be throughly stirred, the sauce and noodles. I do like the place. Not much choices on which makes it easy to make choice and whatever choice will deliver to you yummy foods. Tangsuyuk is very recommendable. I like the texture of the noodles.

author: Seunghu Lee
text: I’m really looking forward to eating Jjajangmeon in Amsterdam. Finally, I’ve been there. Compared to what I ate in Korea, I couldn’t find any difference between them. Not only the taste but also the service of the staff was superb. It felt like I was in Korea. I really recommend it. Try Jjajangmeon! It is the best👍

author: Sharief Kartodikromo
text: A great experience overall. Friendly staff, fast service and good food. I would like some better side dishes like the pickled radish, maybe add some pickled cucumbers. But cant complain. I can only imagine that they will be improving.

author: Mattie Elisa Keessen
text: The first branch in Europe of a chain restaurant in Korea! Didn’t like the noodles as much - but loved the food, took me right back to Korea! Disclaimer: they don’t adjust any food to allergies - do not come here as a vegan/vegetarian or with any allergies :)

author: Florentin Devina
text: Jjajangmyeon is delicious.. but Tangsuyuk is not the style I prefer, still delish though. Their style is heavy on the starch, making it crispy on the outside (as it should) and chewy on the inside (almost mochi like). The meat is thinly …

author: Aeron jong
text: This was my first time trying Korean/Chinese fastfood, at first i was kinda skeptical but the lovely workers there helped me choose my dishes, the food/service was amazing. I recommend this to everyone wanting to try this cuisine.

author: Samuel Budhi Setyanto
text: Jajangmyeon was pretty good, but not special. Beware the portion is huge. But the must try is their tangsuyuk.

author: HyeonJeong Ro
text: I thought the food could been better. Too little meat in noodle dishes and tang-su-yuk was not crispy. But was not a bad experience. Fairly priced and good service.

author: Jenny Cheung
text: I truly don't understand the negative reviews. The service was good and everyone was really friendly. They just opened the restaurant and are still in try out period. It is Korean Chinese cuisine, please keep this in mind when you compare. My sister and I shared Jajang Bap and Gochu Yuringi and it was really delicious! I am still thinking about the Gochu Yuringi, it was really crispy and the sauce was amazing. Will come back soon to try the other dishes. Can't wait for other meat or more vegetarian options. Keep up the good work! Fighting!

author: Miki Liu
text: Delicious Jjajang noodles in Amsterdam! Be sure to try the other dishes too, we loved the Yuringi with spicy pepper dipping sauce =)

author: Tristan V
text: I wanted to give this specific location a better score than it deserved, but after reading the response from the owner, I could not let their entirely incorrect assertions go unchallenged. No, jjajangmyeon* is not difficult to make nor especially to make good. The ingredients themselves should do that. Anyone can see from the photos provided that the lack of anything other than onions (and a few peas) is apparent. Also, can you confirm that jjajang at the korean standard should taste overtly of starch thickener? Should it be burnt and not have umami? What's more is that multiple koreans who have even gone to Paiks noodles in Korea have left reviews stating the food quality is worse here. I advise this owner to learn to read reviews, and instead of responding to them, maybe just improve the food. It is also a ridiculous assertion they provide regarding phones. All outlets are intentionally closed, and when asked if the phone could be charged where the employees have their phones, that was also denied. Funny how you also blame the employees who were nothing but kind about the situation, when it was their manager who was not accommodating. Please read the original review to see how multiple other expectations cannot be met at this restaurant. Original review: I giving the restaurant an overall 3 because I really love korean food and wish to see this restaurant bring more popularity to the cuisine in the Netherlands. The reality is, however, that the food deserves at most 2 stars, and to anyone reading this: be warned. I will tell my friends and family not to go here. Jjajangmyeon is one of the simplest dishes to make at home and to make taste good, and we ordered it to get a baseline understanding of the restaurant. It is also one of the first korean dishes I made and the one I always serve first to introduce people to the cuisine. The jjajangmyeon here was a disgrace. It had no depth of flavor or umami and tasted only of excessive amounts of starch thickener. The dish also tasted burnt, which could be due to the cooking of the jjajang paste or the tough, dry, and sparse pork pieces. There are also usually more vegetables, but this only had onion and a few cabbage pieces. I can't imagine why, other than cutting costs, but that seems to be an overall theme to this restaurant. The portion was average for 1 person, but it is higher priced. We also ordered the Tangsuyuk (sweet and sour pork), which had good thick fried batter, but the sauce served could have been bought in a jar at the grocery store. And why skimp on the wood ear mushrooms and other vegetables, I don't know. Everything was served with a side of a few raw onion pieces with jjajang and pickled cabbage, which is a nice touch. Lastly, the hot teas were served at different temperatures, with the barley being boiling hot and bitter and the corn being lukewarm and bland. The service was great, and the staff was exceptionally friendly. The wait was also not long on a Saturday night. However, why would a restaurant where you need to order via phone (and a physical menu without drink options...) forbid cellphones to be charged using their outlets? It seems that the restaurant is trying in every manner to cut costs, but sadly, this has greatly ruined the quality and experience. Seeing as Paik Jong-won is the face of the business, it is disappointing that his passion for flavor above all else is completely disregarded at his restaurant.

author: Florentin Devina
text: Jjajangmyeon is delicious.. but Tangsuyuk is not the style I prefer, still delish though. Their style is heavy on the starch, making it crispy on the outside (as it should) and chewy on the inside (almost mochi like). The meat is thinly sliced, super soft and mixed in the heavy batter. The portion is generous considering the Amsterdam price.

author: Loulou G.
text: I was really looking forward to eating at this noodle restaurant with my friends. Unfortunately, it turned out that there is absolutely no dish on the menu that does not contain pork. Since I am severely allergic to pork, this was extremely disappointing for me. I would have appreciated it a lot if there had been at least one vegetarian option on the menu, or even just a dish without pork or pork fat. Nowadays, I think it is quite important for restaurants to consider the wide variety of people and dietary needs, and offering at least one vegetarian dish should really be possible. Because of this, I was not able to eat there with my friends, even though I was really excited about it. The only reason I am giving one star is because the waitress was very friendly and immediately informed us that there was no dish without pork, which we were not aware of at first. We honestly assumed there would definitely be at least one option available.

author: Gosia
text: I managed to try it out during the soft opening. Disclaimer: don’t go there super hungry because first you need to wait in line for the table (I went at 5pm and waited a little over 30min) and then for food another ~30min. I had a bit of a confusing experience when I ordered via QR but then was told my order was cancelled because I need to wait for the staff to let me order. I thought I might need to give up cause I was in a bit of a rush but the lovely staff managed to get me the food before I had to run. I ordered Jajangmyeon - it was ok but very little meat and the one that was there was overcooked. The sauce lacked a bit of the flavor that the dish is known for. That being said the staff is trying really hard to provide a great experience and it shows. I’m sure that by the time they open officially everything will work smoothly! I’ll be back to try other dishes.

author: Mary H
text: We visit this place several times and it never dissappoint. Staff is friendly good manners, feels at home and welcome. Really easy to order and the food I give it a 100.

author: King
text: The jajangmyeon tasted really bland. Raw onions were the only thing that would make it somewhat enjoyable. I couldn't finish the bowl, it was not enjoyable. EDIT: Thank you for your response. The best jjajangmyeon's I've had so far had a quite pronounced garlic flavour. If you could also add chillies as a table topping I would love to come back for another try.

author: S.K. Chon
text: I don't recommend this restaurant. It does not use good ingredients and the dish is not fresh. This restaurant has to improve its quality.

author: Rafael Torres
text: Good quality food, the portions are very big so you can easily share. Price is reasonable for the size and quality of the ingredients. They close at 2:30 pm and open again at 4:00 pm but if you arrive and order before 2:30 you can continue eating the food after 2:30

author: Namu Hwang
text: The staff were amazingly kind. The foods are exactly the same in Korea and I’m amazed how they could bring the original taste to Amsterdam! I waited 20 min earlier to the opening and there was already a line of around 6 people. I guess it’s getting so popular. I don’t really suggest visiting when you’re super starving for now. But was it worthy to wait that early? Yes, I tried Jajangmyeon, Jjamppong rice, yuringi and tangsuyuk. I had never been that satisfied in a res in Amsterdam! yuringi sauce was extremely good also. I suggest to order tangsuyuk and yuringi both and dip tangsuyuk into yuringi sauce🌟

author: Klaas Barends
text: Finally a Korean restaurant in downtown where Amsterdam doesn’t have to feel embarrassed about (I am looking at you all-you-can-eat fakes). Authentic Korean taste for a reasonable price. Friendly people that make you feel welcome. At the moment the place to go when you crave real Korean comfort food.

author: Roy Methorst
text: Arrived 20 minutes before opening, already a small line, got in with the first batch. You can order through the QR on the table and pay directly. The food is really good and authentic. It’s totally worth the wait.

author: Hyunji Kim
text: I have to say that my expectation was lower after seeing some reviews here, but I personally liked it very much. I ordered the spicy seafood noodle soup and finished it so quickly because it was delicious - the taste I remember from S.Korea. The noodle was thicker than usual, but the taste was well blended in the soup. The atmosphere is more like a fast food place with bright white lighting. But I had a very nice meal and felt comfy & great afterwards. I'll defo visit again!

author: Nick Sakoff
text: Good ingredients in the soup, but the broth was quite bland, especially for a ‘signature soup’. I would go back to try the other items because the service was great (except for charging for a glass of tap water… isn’t that illegal in Amsterdam?)

author: Wee Tie Kuo
text: First time trying this place in Amsterdam and I must say I was happily surprised. …

author: Emi Gao
text: Had the jajangmyeon and it was alright. It was full of onions and almost no meat unfortunately. Could have been better. The service though, was super quick!

author: Erica K
text: it was sooo Yum <33 btw do you guys not sell tangsooyook anymore? I couldn't find it in online menus😔 …

author: Calcen Chan
text: Really good jjajangmyeon. The side dishes option is simple compared to a typical Korean restaurant.

author: Hendrika
text: Tangsuyuk is delicious, Jajangmyeon is ok Danmuji is not good at all. Service is fast.

author: Dila Korver
text: Honestly I expected much more from the food, it was decent but nothing special to me. I had the signature black bean; there was very little pork, the sauce was good and the noodles were mid. Maybe other dishes are better.

author: Rukai Yin
text: Food is awesome. Absolutely recommend! The restaurant smells, though. It’s a mix of not ventilated air and chemical cleaner.

author: Mert Lenzo
text: Delicious Korean food in Amsterdam seriously, I almost licked the plates clean. The German waitress was the cherry on top: super sweet, explained everything like a pro, 10/10, would come back just for her and the kimchi.

author: youngkwon Jeong (Aiden Jeong)
text: I had the best Korean-style Chinese food experience while traveling in Amsterdam. The jjajangbap, jjamppong, yuringi, and tangsuyuk were all delicious, and the service was great as well.

author: Martijn Muijsers
text: Most disappointing Korean experience in this country yet. Please visit any other establishment instead. …

author: Lorena U
text: The black bean noodles were very good and the portion was big even without upgrading it. I only took a photo once I finished eating. It was delicious.

author: Nii Sowah
text: Tried this korean Chinese place, was my first time,. The blackbean noodles are with enough flavor and i also ordered the ttekbokki that the staff recommended, that was super tasty. Staff is friendly and very helpfull giving recommendations to what to eat as my first time eating Korean food. Will come again to this gem.

author: Joon Ho Hong
text: We recently visited this restaurant, intrigued by its bold claim as a premier spot for authentic Korean-Chinese noodles in Europe, it was not close to the best compared to famous places in Germany. Service was disorganized, with dishes …

author: Jaejin Lee
text: I am Korean living in the UK. I visited Amsterdam and had this food. I was SO impressed that I could have this authentic jjajang noodle in the Netherlands. Please Paik noodle expand its restaurant to the UK!!!

author: Huzeyfe KIRAN
text: You open a place in Netherlands and you say ‘No Halal or Vegetarian options available’. How come? Why? Well your lost. All korean family can’t visit you because we cannot eat together there. I am really disappointed ☹️

author: Mae Cee
text: Dining in Korea feel. Food was great! Staff and service were efficient! Will come back again for sure.

author: Hannah Dev
text: Was warmly welcomed by staff. They were attentive and social. Wait time for food to arrive was around 10 minutes. Portion size was good and loved the little self serve area for pickled radish and onion. Jajamyeong was delicious and up to the expectations set. Felt like a true Seoul diner. Much appreciated.

author: Sergio
text: I don't understand the hype. The jjamppong was too bland and just tasted spicy. They put literally two mussels, two squid rings and two strips of super tough meat. The jjajang sauce was better, but not really different from what you can find elsewhere. I was expecting some wok hei. The fried stuff looked good. I don't think it's worth waiting for one hour (at least) for this.

author: Erica K
text: it was sooo Yum <33 btw do you guys not sell tangsooyook anymore? I couldn't find it in online menus😔

author: Liz
text: Loved the jjajyangmyun, but the jjamppong was rather bland. Order and pay through the QR code on the table.

author: Shuper Panda
text: we had jjajjangmyeon, jjampong & rose deokbeokkie and wow it was so crazy good!! we ordered take away and the service was super nice!! it felt like i was back in south korea.

author: Penny N.
text: Great location and it’s easy to order food from QR code! The food is good and you can get free pickles

author: Özge Demir
text: The food was very delicious and the service really good. The staff was so kind and before ordering this girl even explained to me where to find the cutlery and gave me food recommendations when I asked. I would definitely recommend eating there.

author: Jake Kim
text: As a Korean American living in Europe, I really missed Korean-Chinese cuisine. This place delivered the flavor. Pepper ones are actually spicy enough to make my nose run and noodles had a good texture as well. Very efficient ordering system if you have a smartphone for QR code.

author: p mk
text: the sweet and sour pork (tangsuyuk) is supposed to be light white-colored and crispy, but it came out brownish, with a hard texture and a burnt taste. When it's busy, the oil should be changed every 2 to 3 days, but it seems like they tried to save money by using the same oil for too long. If the one of the basic food tangsuyuk is like this, I can only imagine what the other dishes are like. It might be worth recommending on the day they change the oil. But who’s supposed to know when that is?

author: Alex Petrosidis
text: Junk food. Soup on the menu supposed to have pork and squid and see food but had just 3 small mussels and the rest just cabbage in poor broth with too much chilly . Noodle with black beans just tasted burned onions

author: Sien
text: Food was very authentic and the staff was really nice. Also the signatures of RM was a really nice touch for us army fans

author: Phoebe VP
text: Just wish that they serve drink with a cup or some ice, the place is well organized and food was just right! recommend very much

author: Wee Tie Kuo
text: First time trying this place in Amsterdam and I must say I was happily surprised. Got both signature dish Jajangmyeon and Jjamppong as take out and they were super tasty! I will come back for sure.

author: Joon Ho Hong
text: We recently visited this restaurant, intrigued by its bold claim as a premier spot for authentic Korean-Chinese noodles in Europe, it was not close to the best compared to famous places in Germany. Service was disorganized, with dishes arriving in an inconsistent order, and a one of diner’s frustration added to an uncomfortable atmosphere for us. While the jjampong soup stood out with its smoky, flavorful broth, the other dishes were disappointing. The jjajangmyeon lacked the richness and depth of flavor you expect from authentic versions; the black bean sauce was overly sweety but lacked the savory, umami punch typical of high-quality chunjang. Similarly, the tangsuyuk was underwhelming. The pork was soggy rather than crispy, which ruined the contrast you normally expect between the crunchy exterior and the tender meat. The sauce was also overly sweet and lacked the balance of sweet and tangy that makes tangsuyuk enjoyable. On the positive side, the presentation was appealing, and the prices were reasonable for a restaurant in the city center. The option for unlimited onions and pickled radish was a nice touch, but the taste of the food and service ultimately fell short of expectations. For now, it’s hard to recommend this place, though it may improve with time.

author: Ngoc LHB Nguyen
text: This was the 2nd time I tried the food here and still I was not happy. I tried Paik’s black bean noodles in Seoul and it was mind-blowing. The black bean noodles in the Amsterdam branch is just bland. This 2nd time I tried the seafood noodles and it was disappointed. It had little seafood: 2-3 small rings of squid, 5-6 mussels with shelf on and didnt have much mussels meat inside, 3-4 small pieces of meat, a lot of veggie. The fried pork with sweet and sour sauce was good though. That was it. I really really like Paik’s in Seoul so I was super excited to know that they opened one in Amsterdam. But it did not live up to the quality and flavor as in Seoul. I understand they might want to customize to the Dutch taste but no matter what you do, food still needs to taste good. But this is not the case I’m afraid. I made this review hoping that the Amsterdam branch will read it and improve.

author: Swey L.
text: Visited March 2025. Food was definitely not good in our opinion. Had some spicy noodles that didn't have enough sauce, was watery and literally had 3 tiny strips of pork in them with a whole lot of onions for some reason. Had some fried …

author: Shao-ling Chang
text: When you miss authentic Korean food and it can be found in Amsterdam, you go! Was just as I remembered in Seoul

author: Marcus Hitchens
text: What a amazing place with incredible food . We try most of menu and was super surprised. Taste sooo good . Very friendly helpful personal . Thank you

author: Sion Lee
text: The only perfectly authentic Korean place for reasonable prices in Amsterdam

author: Lekha Samant
text: If you are a fan of Culinary Class War then it’s worth visiting Paik’s noodle atleast once. The food was 7/10 for me. The portion is generous and noodles are cooked to perfection. The ambience of the restaurant felt like retail mcdonald’s or kfc outlets. Apart from the serving of the main dish, everything else is a sort of self service.

author: Hannah Boom
text: The food that we got was cold, we sent it back and got another lukewarm dish like it was already waiting to be served for a while. The fried food was hot but overall the taste of the dishes have no depth to it. There are way better places …

author: Par Par
text: Very Korean type restaurant, I had jajangmyeon in Korea once and it tastes similar, also nice portions

author: Clara Murakami
text: Finally tried Paik’s Noodle in Amsterdam, it’s almost like in Korea. You have to keep in mind prices are much higher in the Netherlands compared to South Korea. But still considering the location it’s very reasonable. I mean some sandwiches in the city are much more expensive that’s this bowl of noodles, it’s super weird. I really enjoyed the gochu jjajangmyeon in combination with the Yuringu, the sauces are so addicting. Service is fast and friendly which is rare in the city, this is a big plus.

author: Naoual Chouhou
text: The entire experience was amazing, took me right back to Korea. Me and my friend agreed that the interior reminded us of eating at 김가네, banged my knee into the cutlery drawer but what’s new. Side dishes are self serve, good to know for next …

author: Reia
text: I visited here three times and the first two times I was quite disappointed by the overly salty soup and dried-out texture of tangsuyuk meat. BUT I recently went there again and the taste IMPROVED A LOT! 😋 …

author: YY
text: The two of us had a large jjamppong and a gochu mayo chicken. They definitely don't skimp on the portion sizes of their size-up noodles, 1 size-up is enough for two people. The jjamppong could have some more seafood in it though. Gochu mayo chicken was very flavourful. A good spot to go to for jjamppong and/or jjajangmyeon.

author: Daniels Lavrentjevs
text: Hands down the best noodles I’ve had in Amsterdam!! Service was fast and friendly. Highly recommended!

author: Rania Radwan
text: Lovely music and service! Staff was very friendly and helpful and food was delicious very reasonable prices for city centre! A meal and a drink for under 20 euros

author: cathlyn escarlan
text: Gochu Jjamppong 고추짬뽕 deserves 5 stars. Was truly delicious. thing is we orderer other noodles and they came literally with 2 stripes of small pork and maybe two or 3 tiny shrimp. i was very dissapointed when we saw that. was hard to eat because the dish itself its quite big. but making it full of veggies and almost no meat turns it into almost a vegan option. the restaurant is clean and tidy.

author: SimpleJaylee
text: The restaurant is easily accessible and easy to find; with a wide selection of dishes, delicious food, and friendly staff. I enjoyed my first experience at Paik's Noodle, and I will definitely be back.

author: Koen Hendriks
text: We were expecting great things from the first of its kind, Paik’s Noodle in Amsterdam. We came to Amsterdam just for the purpose of eating here. First of all, the 탕수육 was very good, sticky sauce and nicely fried pork. Secondly, the 짜장면 was nice however it was not the same type of noodle that is commonly used in Korea for this type of dish, that’s why it did not reach our expected level of authenticity, nonetheless the flavors were good. The food & price quality did not disappoint. Lastly, however one main point of feedback would be to close off the toilet facility upstairs, the toilet/cleaning smell upstairs was rather strong and it negatively affected our appetite&experience. We hope to come back next time and be able to give our experience 5 stars!

author: Sung-ho Lee
text: Sub par compared to the other Paik's franchise. Noodle was over cooked. Sauce is not even close to the Korean standard. Over priced too.

author: William
text: Can’t believe I just paid the amount I did for that food (16 + 15 euro’s for two people. It looked and tasted like an instant noodle pack. The taste was bland, and the supposed sides’ you get could get, were literally raw onions or pickled …

author: ana m.
text: Such yummy food! And super lovely staff. I’ve been looking forward to come by since following you guys on instagram this summer and I will definitely be coming back:)

author: Chocobo
text: Ordering is through QR code. Portions were big and tasty

author: N K
text: Finally a decent authentic korean restaurant in Amsterdam. Would love to visit again!

author: Sarah Alonso
text: From the moment we walked in, you could smell how delicious it was going to be. Honestly one of the best Korean-Chinese restaurants I have had in Europe! We will come back! <3 Will stand on that line for as long as it takes because it’s so worth it!

author: YesFather
text: Service was fast. Foods are delicious. Only thing I would like them to lower Soju price, because it is 1€ in Korea, 4€ in Netherlands markets (amazing oriental), and here is 15€. It is my personal wish to see Soju price for 6-7€, so I could enjoy drinking too.

author: LEH
text: We waited around 45min for a group of six people around 17:15. Not too bad around dinner time. …

author: Peter
text: I got the Jajangmyun, jjamppong and the tangsuyuk. Honestly, the food was quite bland. Jajangmyun could've had more deeper flavor of chunjang and jjamppong had the fire taste meaning they have good technique but it wasn't spicy at all. The best were the tangsuyuk and the onions for the side dish but both of them dissapointed in portions. Maybe I was too hyped, but it was a dissapointing experience considering the long wait/hype for the restaurant to open. Let me clarify on my review. By the absence of deeper flavor, I meant that the jajangmyun was void of savoriness and just sweet. Maybe it was a one-off thing, but as I said, pretty dissapointing.

author: Jay Kim
text: Jajangmyeon was bit sweet side but it looks like they put a lot of onions. Jjampong was spicy and bit salty but that is how it should taste. Also Tang Su Yuk, the batter was good and sauce tasted just like Korea. We will come back soon!

author: Choon Seng Lim
text: The fried pork is too starchy.

author: Lisa
text: I was expecting the noodles to be salty but they were rather sweet. It was still tasty regardless.

author: Ralph Kim
text: I can highly recommend Paik's Noodles. The staff is friendly as always. Think ir is the 9th time i was there to eat. Food tastes same as in South Korea. I would say, give it a try.

author: Maosheng
text: Ordered a Bokkeum Jjamppong, received noodles with 2 small pieces of pork, 3 tiny shrimp pieces and 2 very thin calamari… Not worth your money. Ordered jajangmyeon aswell but the taste is not that good

author: Inten Tirkasari
text: Love the spicy jajangmeon and that noodle with seafood (forget the name). Toally recommended. Oh, thay also have a very nice tangsuyuk

author: Albert YANG
text: All of the noodles are far from authentic Korean foods except Tangsuyuk.

author: Cansu Bölükbaşı
text: We were immediately repelled by the smell when we step inside because it smelled like a toilet. We ordered 3 different kind of mains. The food was nothing special, it was kinda bland and overcooked.

The restaurant was disappointing. The fried dishes were okay, but the noodle dishes were subpar. The sauce tasted bland and underseasoned, and the noodles didn’t pair well with the dish. Although the fried dishes came out quickly, the noodles took quite a while to be served.

author: Marina Giacosa Esnal
text: The food was absolutely delicious. We had the Jajang myeon, the Jjamppong, and the Tang suyuk, and it was just amazing. We were both super full, and the person I went with is Korean and he was super happy and thought the food tasted just like at home ✨✨

author: Martijn Muijsers
text: Most disappointing Korean experience in this country yet. Please visit any other establishment instead. The staff are friendly and try to help, but the strict rules of this restaurant essentially prohibit them from being very helpful. They have no vegetarian options, but also no pescatarian options: every meal contains meat, even those mainly based on fish. Since strictly no food can be brought in, this makes it really hard to dine here as a group. In addition, their additional costs for takeaway are quite high. Ordering is with QR and an app. The system asks for a tip before being able to pay. This is dislikeable and already prompted me to not return here even before having the food. Takeaway boxes are made of a type of black plastic that is not recycled in the Netherlands (essentially, only burnable). The food is very disappointing. While there was nothing bad about it, there is also barely any good about it. It cannot beat many similar pre-made packages available at any Amazing Oriental in both taste and quantity of ingredients. While it may be made with some special recipe and fresh ingredients, the end result is just not great: the taste is nice but too weak and unnecessarily sweet, and there is barely any meat or seafood in any of the dishes. The tangsuyuk and gochu yuringi are the "good" options on the menu, but they are typically better at Korean restaurants that specialize in fried dishes, and they are too expensive for what you get. The place itself is devoid of any atmosphere whatsoever. It is very clean though, the staff does a good job at cleaning. In summary: staff is good, ownership can use a real hint that it is 2025.

author: Hannah Boom
text: The food that we got was cold, we sent it back and got another lukewarm dish like it was already waiting to be served for a while. The fried food was hot but overall the taste of the dishes have no depth to it. There are way better places to get Korean food in Amsterdam. Also they didn’t tell us where to find the side dishes, where to find the chop sticks. We had to ask for everything. A little explanation would be nice.

author: Naoual Chouhou
text: The entire experience was amazing, took me right back to Korea. Me and my friend agreed that the interior reminded us of eating at 김가네, banged my knee into the cutlery drawer but what’s new. Side dishes are self serve, good to know for next time! The food was great and you get large portions for a good price. Overall an amazing meal and atmosphere, will definitely come back here!

author: William
text: Can’t believe I just paid the amount I did for that food (16 + 15 euro’s for two people. It looked and tasted like an instant noodle pack. The taste was bland, and the supposed sides’ you get could get, were literally raw onions or pickled radish. It looks like there was no effort at all to make the food worth the price. I ordered the classic black beans noodles and the spicy variant.

author: LEH
text: We waited around 45min for a group of six people around 17:15. Not too bad around dinner time. The interieur was simplistic with cutlery in the table. Staff was fast and prompt but the tables weren’t set properly with plates and banchan until we asked for them. Jajangmyeon - black bean noodles 9/10 They were al dente, flavor was spot on and the right consistency. You can’t go wrong with choosing this dish. Jajang Bap - black bean rice with egg 7/10 The rice was a bit hard, the black bean was flavored well and the egg was good. The soup that accompanied it wasn’t necessary. Better to not have the soup. Bokkeum Jjamppong - stir fried noodles with meat and seafood 6.5/10 This dish was bland and not as filled with meat and seafood as the picture showed. It wasn’t really spicy, so really reflects the one pepper sign. Tangsuyuk - deep fried pork with sweet sour sauce 7.5/10 Well flavored, not too hard fried. But it gets soggy really quick if you wait due to the sauce. Gochu Yuringi - fried chicken 8.5/10 This dish was simple but well executed and the flavor was really good. Together with the sauce and salad with cucumber was refreshing. Recommended dish! Overall, recommend this place. If you don’t like spicy food, there are three dishes to choose. So the choice for people that can’t handle spice is very limited. Just pick the black bean noodles and the fried chicken dish.

author: Nik Niks
